steps.subgoalLabel: Pick a topic and your opinion
steps.instruction: Choose something you have an opinion about. What do you think?
steps.work: Topic: seasons. My opinion: Summer is the best season.
steps.explanation: An opinion is what you think or believe. Not everyone has to agree with you.

steps.instruction: Write one sentence that tells your opinion clearly.
steps.work: I think summer is the best season.
steps.explanation: Starting with 'I think' makes it clear this is your opinion. The reader knows right away what you believe.

steps.instruction: Tell why you have this opinion. Use the word 'because.'
steps.work: I think summer is the best season because I can swim and play outside.
steps.explanation: The word 'because' connects your opinion to your reason. Reasons help people understand why you think that way.

steps.instruction: Write one more sentence to end your writing.
steps.work: I think summer is the best season because I can swim and play outside. Summer is so much fun!
steps.explanation: The closing sentence wraps up your opinion. 'Summer is so much fun!' gives the reader a strong ending.

steps.instruction: Think about a kind of pet. Which pet do you think is the best?
steps.work: My opinion: Cats are the best pets.
steps.explanation: Your opinion is your own idea. Someone else might think dogs are the best, and that is okay.

steps.instruction: Write a sentence that clearly tells your opinion.
steps.work: I think cats are the best pets.
steps.explanation: This sentence is clear. The reader knows exactly what you believe.

steps.instruction: Tell one reason why you think this. Use 'because.'
steps.work: I think cats are the best pets because they are soft and cuddly.
steps.explanation: 'Because they are soft and cuddly' gives the reader a reason to understand your opinion.

steps.instruction: Write a sentence to finish your opinion piece.
steps.work: I think cats are the best pets because they are soft and cuddly. Everyone should have a cat!
steps.explanation: The closing sentence 'Everyone should have a cat!' is a strong way to end. It shows you really believe your opinion.
